If an environmental impact review is ordered under subparagraph 128(1)(b)(i), paragraph 128(1)(c) or 130(1)(a), subparagraph 130(1)(b)(ii) or paragraph 131(1)(b) in respect of a proposal for a development that, as determined by the Review Board, is to be carried out partly outside the Mackenzie Valley and either is to be carried out partly in Wekeezhii or might have an impact on the environment in Wekeezhii, the Review Board shall enter into an agreement for the purpose of jointly establishing a review panel and prescribing the manner of its examination of the impact on the environment of the development

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: with an authority responsible for the examination of environmental effects of the part of the development that is to be carried out outside the Mackenzie Valley; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: with the Minister of the Environment if that Minister is authorized under section 40 of the Canadian Environmental Assessment Act to enter into such an agreement.

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (3), if, within the period fixed by the regulations, an agreement has not been entered into under that subsection, a panel of the Review Board shall conduct an environmental impact review of the development, but the review shall be limited to the part of the development to be carried out in the Mackenzie Valley.
A review panel or joint panel established by an agreement referred to in subsection (2) or (3) shall make a report of its examination to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the federal Minister, who shall distribute it to every responsible minister;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any designated regulatory agency from which a licence, permit or other authorization is required for the carrying out of the development;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a joint panel referred to in paragraph (2)(b) or (3)(a), the minister of the federal, provincial or territorial government having jurisdiction in relation to examinations conducted by the authority referred to in that paragraph; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the Tlicho Government, if the development is to be carried out partly on Tlicho lands.

Subsection.Text: An examination by a review panel or joint panel referred to in subsection (2) or (3) stands in lieu of an environmental impact review of the proposal referred to in that subsection and paragraphs 134(1)(b), (d) and (e) and sections 135 to 137.2 apply, with such modifications as may be required, in respect of the examination, except that a recommendation of a panel may not be referred back to the panel for further consideration.
The portion of subsection 143(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

The Governor in Council may, following consultation by the federal Minister with the territorial Minister, first nations and the Tlicho Government, make regulations for carrying out the purposes and provisions of this Part and, in particular, regulations
Subsection 143(1) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “and” at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: fixing a period for the purposes of subsection 138.1(2) and respecting mediation referred to in that subsection;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: fixing the period within which a matter may be referred to arbitration under subsection 138.1(3) and respecting arbitration under that subsection; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: fixing a period for the purposes of subsections 138.1(4) and 141(4).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 143(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Regulations may only be made under paragraph (1)(a), (d), (e), (f) or (g), or amended under paragraph (1)(b) or (c), following consultation by the federal Minister with the Review Board.

Section.Text: Subsection 144(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, by regulations made following consultation by the federal Minister with the territorial Minister, the Review Board, the first nations and the Tlicho Government, amend the schedule by adding, or by deleting, the name of any agency, other than a land and water board established by Part 3 or 4, that exercises regulatory powers pursuant to territorial or federal laws and that is not subject to specific control or direction by a minister of the federal or territorial government or the Governor in Council.

Section.Text: Section 147 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Consultation with first nations and Tlicho Government

Subsection.Text: A responsible authority that is a minister of the Crown in right of Canada shall carry out the functions referred to in section 146 in consultation with the first nations and the Tlicho Government.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Role of first nations and Tlicho Government
Subsection.Text: Where a responsible authority is other than a minister of the Crown in right of Canada, the Gwich’in and Sahtu First Nations and the Tlicho Government are entitled to participate in the functions referred to in section 146 in the manner provided by the regulations.
Subsection 148(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The federal Minister shall, after consulting the Gwich’in First Nation, the Sahtu First Nation, the Tlicho Government and the territorial government, fix the terms of reference of an environmental audit, including the key components of the environment to be examined.
Subsection 148(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Participation by first nations and Tlicho Government
Subsection.Text: The Gwich’in and Sahtu First Nations and the Tlicho Government are entitled to participate in an environmental audit in the manner provided by the regulations.
The portion of section 150 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may, after consultation by the federal Minister with affected first nations, the Tlicho Government and the territorial Minister, make regulations for carrying out the purposes and provisions of this Part and, in particular, regulations

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 150(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the manner of participation of the Gwich’in and Sahtu First Nations and the Tlicho Government in the functions of a responsible authority that is not a minister of the Crown or in an environmental audit.

Subsection.Text: The Wekeezhii Land and Water Board established by section 57.1 of the Mackenzie Valley Resource Management Act, as enacted by section 31 of this Act, may not exercise its powers or perform its duties under sections 58.1 and 59, subsections 60(1) and (2), sections 79.1 to 79.3, 80.1 and 88 and subsection 89(2) of the Mackenzie Valley Resource Management Act until six months after the coming into force of this Act.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Mackenzie Valley Land and Water Board
Subsection.Text: Despite subsection 102(1) of the Mackenzie Valley Resource Management Act, the Mackenzie Valley Land and Water Board shall exercise the powers and perform the duties of the Wekeezhii Land and Water Board under sections 58.1, 79.1 to 79.3, 80.1 and 88 and subsection 89(2) of that Act during the period of six months after the coming into force of this Act.

Despite subsection 32(1) of the Mackenzie Valley Resource Management Act and section 18 of the Federal Courts Act, the Supreme Court of the Northwest Territories has exclusive original jurisdiction to hear and determine any action or proceeding, whether or not by way of an application of a type referred to in subsection 32(1) of the Mackenzie Valley Resource Management Act, concerning the jurisdiction of the Wekeezhii Land and Water Board during the period of six months after the coming into force of this Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Validity of ordinances of the Northwest Territories
Section.Text: The following ordinances of the Northwest Territories are deemed for all purposes to have been validly made if they were made before the coming into force of this Act and would have been validly made if they had been made after that coming into force, and everything done under any of those ordinances before that coming into force has the effect that it would otherwise have if the ordinance had been validly made after that coming into force:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the ordinance of the Northwest Territories entitled the Tlicho Land Claims and Self-Government Agreement Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an ordinance that establishes community governments as required by chapter 8 of the Agreement; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an ordinance that establishes a community services agency as required by the first intergovernmental services agreement referred to in 7.10 of chapter 7 of the Agreement.

Section.Text: Subsection 13(3) of the Access to Information Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The expression aboriginal government in paragraph (1)(e) means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: Nisga’a Government, as defined in the Nisga’a Final Agreement given effect by the Nisga’a Final Agreement Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Tlicho Government, as defined in section 2 of the Tlicho Land Claims and Self-Government Act.

Section.Text: Paragraph 24(1)(a) of the Canada Lands Surveys Act is amended by striking out the word “or” at the end of subparagraph (iv), by replacing the word “and” at the end of subparagraph (v) with the word “or” and by adding the following after subparagraph (v):

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: Tlicho lands, as defined in section 2 of the Mackenzie Valley Resource Management Act; and

Section.Text: Section 40 of the Canadian Environmental Assessment Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2.1):

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (2.1), if, in respect of a proposal referred to in subsection 138.1(1) of the Mackenzie Valley Resource Management Act, no agreement is entered into under that subsection within the period fixed by the regulations referred to in subsection 138.1(4) of that Act, an assessment by a review panel of the proposal shall be conducted.

The Minister shall to the extent possible ensure that any assessment of the proposal required by subsection (2.2) is coordinated with any environmental impact review of the proposal under the Mackenzie Valley Resource Management Act.

Before taking a course of action under subsection 37(1) in respect of a proposal referred to in subsection (2.3), the responsible authority shall take into consideration any report in respect of the proposal that is issued under subsection 134(2) of the Mackenzie Valley Resource Management Act and shall consult the persons and bodies to whom the report is submitted or distributed under subsection 134(3) of that Act.

Section.Text: Subsection 4(1) of the Lobbyists Registration Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (d.2):

Paragraph.Label: (d.3)
Paragraph.Text: members or employees of the Tlicho Government, as defined in section 2 of the Tlicho Land Claims and Self-Government Act, or persons on the staff of those members;

Section.Text: The Northwest Territories Act is amended by adding the following after section 16:

Section.MarginalNote: Roads identified in Tlicho Land Claims and Self-Government Agreement
Section.Text: Ordinances made by the Commissioner in Council under paragraph 16(o) apply to roads identified in the Agreement, as defined in section 2 of the Tlicho Land Claims and Self-Government Act, as if they were on public lands if the Agreement provides that those ordinances apply to those roads.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 17:

Despite section 17, the Commissioner in Council may, in exercising the powers of the Commissioner in Council under section 16 for the purpose of implementing the Agreement, as defined in section 2 of the Tlicho Land Claims and Self-Government Act, make ordinances that are in relation to the matters coming within class 24 of section 91 of the Constitution Act, 1867.
The Northwest Territories Waters Act is amended by adding the following after section 9:

Subsection.Text: Sections 8 and 9 do not apply in respect of a use of waters or a deposit of waste in a Tlicho community, if the local government of that community has enacted a bylaw providing that a licence is not required for that type of use or deposit.
The expressions Tlicho community and local government in subsection (1) have the same meaning as in section 2 of the Mackenzie Valley Resource Management Act.

Section.Text: Section 14 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (6):

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, licences issued by the Board under this Act either before or after the coming into force of this subsection are not statutory instruments within the meaning of the Statutory Instruments Act.

Heading.TitleText: Payments in Lieu of Taxes Act

Section.Text: The definition taxing authority in subsection 2(1) of the Payments in Lieu of Taxes Act is amended by striking out the word “or” at the end of paragraph (e), by adding the word “or” at the end of paragraph (f) and by adding the following after paragraph (f):

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the Tlicho Government, as defined in section 2 of the Tlicho Land Claims and Self-Government Act, if it levies and collects a real property tax or a frontage or area tax in respect of Tlicho lands, as defined in section 2 of the Mackenzie Valley Resource Management Act.

Section.Text: Subsection 8(7) of the Privacy Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The expression aboriginal government in paragraph (2)(k) means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: Nisga’a Government, as defined in the Nisga’a Final Agreement given effect by the Nisga’a Final Agreement Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Tlicho Government, as defined in section 2 of the Tlicho Land Claims and Self-Government Act.

Section.Text: On the later of the coming into force of section 16 of the Westbank First Nation Self-Government Act and section 97 of this Act, subsection 13(3) of the Access to Information Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The expression aboriginal government in paragraph (1)(e) means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: Nisga’a Government, as defined in the Nisga’a Final Agreement given effect by the Nisga’a Final Agreement Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the council, as defined in the Westbank First Nation Self-Government Agreement given effect by the Westbank First Nation Self-Government Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Tlicho Government, as defined in section 2 of the Tlicho Land Claims and Self-Government Act.

Section.Text: On the later of the coming into force of section 17 of the Westbank First Nation Self-Government Act and section 100 of this Act, paragraph 4(1)(d.3) of the Lobbyists Registration Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d.3)
Paragraph.Text: members of the council, as defined in the Westbank First Nation Self-Government Agreement given effect by the Westbank First Nation Self-Government Act, or persons on the staff of the council or of a member of the council;

Paragraph.Label: (d.4)
Paragraph.Text: members or employees of the Tlicho Government, as defined in section 2 of the Tlicho Land Claims and Self-Government Act, or persons on the staff of those members;

Section.Text: On the later of the coming into force of subsection 18(2) of the Westbank First Nation Self-Government Act and section 106 of this Act, subsection 8(7) of the Privacy Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The expression aboriginal government in paragraph (2)(k) means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: Nisga’a Government, as defined in the Nisga’a Final Agreement given effect by the Nisga’a Final Agreement Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the council of the Westbank First Nation; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Tlicho Government, as defined in section 2 of the Tlicho Land Claims and Self-Government Act.

Subsection.Text: If subsection 3(1) of An Act to amend the Lobbyists Registration Act, chapter 10 of the Statutes of Canada, 2003 (the “other Act”), comes into force before the coming into force of section 100 of this Act, then, on the later of the coming into force of that subsection 3(1) and the day on which this Act receives royal assent, section 100 of this Act and the heading before it are repealed.

If subsection 3(1) of the other Act comes into force on or after the day on which section 100 of this Act comes into force and section 108 of this Act has not had its effect, then, on the coming into force of that subsection 3(1), paragraph 4(1)(d.3) of the Lobbyists Registration Act is repealed.

If subsection 3(1) of the other Act comes into force on or after the day on which section 100 of this Act comes into force and section 108 of this Act has had its effect, then, on the coming into force of that subsection 3(1), paragraphs 4(1)(d.3) and (d.4) of the Lobbyists Registration Act are repealed.

Section.Text: This Act, other than sections 107 to 110, comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
